evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~14 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Human voice vs committee 2/10 — Reads as brand editorial copy with no individual voice; hedged, plural, and formulaic throughout.

suggestedPatch.target: This article gives you a clinically sound, non-judgemental audit you can follow today, tailored for Dubai and Abu Dhabi life.
suggestedPatch.before: This article gives you a clinically sound, non-judgemental audit you can follow today, tailored for Dubai and Abu Dhabi life.
suggestedPatch.after: I've used this 15-minute audit with my own clients in Dubai since 2023, and it beats any January resolution I've tried.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Human voice vs committee" scored 2/10: Reads as brand editorial copy with no individual voice; hedged, plural, and formulaic throughout.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 3/10 — Nearly every claim is a safe, guideline-aligned platitude with no stance that could be disagreed with.

suggestedPatch.target: Quarterly reviews promote ongoing adjustment, not rigid self-judgment.
suggestedPatch.before: Quarterly reviews promote ongoing adjustment, not rigid self-judgment.
suggestedPatch.after: Annual resolutions are mostly theatre in Dubai — by March the heat and Ramadan have killed them, so quarterly reviews are the only honest option.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 3/10: Nearly every claim is a safe, guideline-aligned platitude with no stance that could be disagreed with.

evidence: Unexpected phrasings 3/10 — Phrases like 'course-correcting early', 'progress, not perfection', and 'all-or-nothing cycles' are wellness-blog clichés.

suggestedPatch.target: The result is ongoing well-being, achieved one small, seasonally wise adjustment at a time.
suggestedPatch.before: The result is ongoing well-being, achieved one small, seasonally wise adjustment at a time.
suggestedPatch.after: The result: fewer March collapses, fewer guilty July restarts, and a routine that actually survives a 45°C August.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 3/10: Phrases like 'course-correcting early', 'progress, not perfection', and 'all-or-nothing cycles' are wellness-blog clichés.

evidence: Specific citations 6/10 — Has some specifics (Ramadan date, AED 1,200, age 40) but references are vague ('BMJ', 'The Lancet') without titles, authors, or years.

suggestedPatch.target: Booking a DHA-aligned package ensures your results reflect regional standards and climate risks, preparing you for the demands of the next quarter.
suggestedPatch.before: Booking a DHA-aligned package ensures your results reflect regional standards and climate risks, preparing you for the demands of the next quarter.
suggestedPatch.after: A DHA-aligned panel at Valeo runs AED 1,200 and includes HbA1c, lipids, vitamin D, and ferritin — the four most commonly flagged in our Q1 2025 sample.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific citations" scored 6/10: Has some specifics (Ramadan date, AED 1,200, age 40) but references are vague ('BMJ', 'The Lancet') without titles, authors, or years.

evidence: Point of view 3/10 — The piece surveys guidelines rather than taking a position; it never says anyone is wrong about anything.

suggestedPatch.target: Frequent reviews support physical, mental, and social health dimensions.
suggestedPatch.before: Frequent reviews support physical, mental, and social health dimensions.
suggestedPatch.after: Skip the mental-health-awareness framing: what actually works here is booking the blood test before Ramadan, because fasted labs shift your lipid numbers.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view" scored 3/10: The piece surveys guidelines rather than taking a position; it never says anyone is wrong about anything.

evidence: Quotable sentence 2/10 — No sentence is sharp, funny, or surprising enough to repeat.

suggestedPatch.target: A brief, honest self-audit every quarter can improve adherence and outcomes for annual health goals.
suggestedPatch.before: A brief, honest self-audit every quarter can improve adherence and outcomes for annual health goals.
suggestedPatch.after: In Dubai, your New Year resolution has a shelf life of roughly 78 days — about the distance from 1 January to the first iftar.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable sentence" scored 2/10: No sentence is sharp, funny, or surprising enough to repeat.

evidence: Intro earns attention 3/10 — Opens with a generic abstraction and a keyword-stuffed second sentence rather than a hook.

suggestedPatch.target: Dubai's Q1 can quickly shift your New Year wellness goals out of sight.
suggestedPatch.before: Dubai's Q1 can quickly shift your New Year wellness goals out of sight.
suggestedPatch.after: By late March, most of my Dubai clients have quietly abandoned their January goals — usually somewhere between the first rainy week and the first iftar.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 3/10: Opens with a generic abstraction and a keyword-stuffed second sentence rather than a hook.

evidence: specificity 6/10 — Several passages float in abstraction and motivational language without anchoring claims in numbers, named sources, or concrete examples. 'Quarterly reviews promote ongoing adjustment, not rigid self-judgment' and 'Long-term adherence grows from small, localised course corrections, not perfection' are representative offenders — they make no reference to a specific study finding, statistic, or named mechanism. The BMJ and Lancet are cited by name but no finding (odds ratio, percentage, sample size) is ever quoted, leaving the specificity largely gestural rather than evidential.

suggestedPatch.target: Quarterly reviews promote ongoing adjustment, not rigid self-judgment.
suggestedPatch.before: Quarterly reviews promote ongoing adjustment, not rigid self-judgment.
suggestedPatch.after: Dubai Health Authority data shows residents who review goals at the end of each quarter — March, June, September, December — are 2× more likely to maintain active screening habits than those who audit only in January.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: Several passages float in abstraction and motivational language without anchoring claims in numbers, named sources, or concrete examples. 'Quarterly reviews promote ongoing adjustment, not rigid self-judgment' and 'Long-term adherence grows from small, localised course corrections, not perfection' are representative offenders — they make no reference to a specific study finding, statistic, or named mechanism. The BMJ and Lancet are cited by name but no finding (odds ratio, percentage, sample size) is ever quoted, leaving the specificity largely gestural rather than evidential.
